nameMeanings.name: Cromwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire, from Old English crumb ‘bent, crooked’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Haldane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Haldan (Old Norse Halfdanr, Half(f)dan, Anglo-Scandinavian Healfdene, literally ‘half Dane’).
Scottish: shortened form of the Lanarkshire surname McCalden, probably from Mac Haldan ‘son of Haldan’, with the same meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Hadden
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Hadden, Halden, or Haulden (Roxburghshire), or a variant of   Haldane  .
Irish (Armagh and Tyrone): though mainly Scottish, this surname is sometimes used as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉidín ‘descendant of Éidín’ (see Hayden ).

variants.name: Caldon
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened and altered form of McCalden, probably from Gaelic Mac Haldan ‘son of Haldan’, see Haldane .

variants.name: Halden
meanings: English: habitational name from High Halden in Kent.

variants.name: Howden
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Howden (Midlothian, Dumfriesshire).

English (Yorkshire and Lincolnshire): habitational name from any of several places called Howden, such as those in East Yorkshire, Durham, Northumberland, and Yorkshire. The East Yorkshire placename derives from Old English hēafod ‘head, top, headland’, later replaced by Old Norse hǫfuth ‘head, headland’, + Old English denu ‘valley’. Many of the other placenames derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + denu.

nameMeanings.name: Alden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alan  , in Britain generally found only as a surname, but in North America equally common as a given name.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Haldane Alden Cromwell was born in 1128, in Kilpeck, Herefordshire, England. He had at least 1 son. He died in Kilpeck, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.
